title: The Cost of Dynamic Reasoning: AI Agents and Test-Time Scaling from an Infrastructure Perspective
preview: Analyses the infrastructure cost implications of test-time compute scaling and chain-of-thought reasoning in production AI deployments.
summary: Quantifies the compute overhead of reasoning models (o1-style) and multi-step agent pipelines relative to standard chat inference. Models the cost of test-time scaling (sampling multiple completions, self-consistency) versus training-time scaling on task quality and per-query cost. Provides guidance for infrastructure teams deciding when reasoning models are cost-effective.
datacenter: Directly relevant to cluster configuration for reasoning-heavy workloads. Test-time scaling dramatically increases tokens-per-query, multiplying GPU-hours per request and requiring different batching and memory strategies than standard chat serving.
metrics: Key result signal: GPU-hours per query ratio between reasoning and standard inference modes across task difficulty levels.
